dungeon: Anubi's Lair (Egyptian tomb) — ROTF; Anubis boss + Pillars mechanic recovered
source: video disc_t8v5IlBPPJk (600 frames, 36 transitions, 150 chat; client X1.0). Lava dungeons + a GOLD SANDSTONE TEMPLE (Anubis's domain) + snow. Chat captured Anubis boss taunts.
map: DOCUMENTED biomes: orange/red LAVA-brick dungeons, a GOLD/SANDSTONE TEMPLE with brown-brick frames + gold diamond-tiled floor (Anubis's lair / Egyptian tomb), white snow, a purple/gold castle arena.

bosses.name: Anubis
taunts: I am losing strength... I am slowly dying...
bosses.note: DOCUMENTED VERBATIM (boss-colored chat). ANUBIS — the Egyptian death-god boss of ANUBI'S LAIR (a ROTF dungeon on the build TODO). Relies on 'PILLARS' (destructible pillar minions it summons / calls for help — parallels Ra's Staves in Gate to the Underworld). Death/weakening lines suggest a phase where it loses strength as its Pillars fall. Gold sandstone temple arena.

mechanics: DOCUMENTED: ANUBI'S LAIR boss = ANUBIS, with a PILLARS mechanic — 'Pillars... please help me!' (Anubis summons/relies on Pillars; 'I am losing strength... I am slowly dying...' implies it weakens as Pillars are destroyed). This MIRRORS the Gate-to-the-Underworld Ra fight (destroy Ra's Staves first) — so Anubi's Lair can be BUILT on the same pattern: Anubis invulnerable/weakening until its Pillars fall. Gold sandstone Egyptian temple biome. Client X1.0.
uncertain: Anubis's exact phase structure inferred from the two taunts (weaken + call Pillars). HP/attacks/drops not isolated. Whether 'Pillars' are destructible like Ra's Staves is inferred (strong parallel). Dungeon name 'Anubi's Lair' assumed from the Anubis boss + Egyptian biome.
confidence: HIGH for the Anubis taunts (verbatim) + the Pillars mechanic + the gold-sandstone Egyptian biome. Anubi's Lair is now BUILDABLE (Anubis + destructible Pillars, on the Gate-to-the-Underworld/Ra pattern). MEDIUM for exact phases/drops.
